steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: “artist Susan Billy typically uses willow— a strong and flexible ______ craft baskets that are both beautiful and elaborate.” An opening em-dash introduces an appositive that interrupts the main clause. The blank must complete the appositive noun and close the interruption so the sentence flows into the infinitive phrase “craft baskets.”

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘material. To’: A period ends the sentence after “material,” leaving “To craft baskets…” as a fragment. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘material. to’: Same problem as A — the period creates a fragment beginning with lowercase “to.” ✗
  • Choice C — ‘material;to’: A semicolon must join two independent clauses, but “to craft baskets” is not an independent clause. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘material—to’: The closing em-dash mirrors the opening em-dash, correctly ending the appositive “a strong and flexible material” and allowing “to craft baskets” to complete the main clause. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
